title: No milestones block — add a milestone block kind (samples catalog + active .context)
description: There is no milestone block kind, yet phases need a checkpoint to roll up into. Add a milestone block. Fields: id (MILE-NNN), name, status (planned | reached, no missed), optional release to group gaps/issues/tasks/phases into a release (e.g. 0.30.1). No intent field. No evidence_block/evidence_query. reached is derived: a milestone is reached when its placed phases are done (placement relation, FGAP-039); stories attach to it via the wildcard relation (FGAP-041). Lands in both the packaged samples catalog (via the pi-context update CLI) and the active .context substrate.

evidence.reference: block_kinds registry lists 17 kinds (decisions, framework-gaps, tasks, verification, issues, features, research, rationale, spec-reviews, layer-plans, requirements, conventions, context-contracts, phase, story, work-orders, session-notes) — none is 'milestone'
phase description references story -> milestone/phase/project-goal edges, but no milestone block kind exists
story description names milestone as a cross-altitude edge target; no milestone block backs it

impact: Phases and stories reference milestones as edge targets with nothing to point at, and there is no block to roll gaps/issues/tasks/phases into a release — milestones cannot be represented in the substrate.
proposed_resolution: Add a milestone block kind with id (MILE-NNN), name, status (planned|reached), optional release; no intent; reached derived from placed phases (FGAP-039); organized by stories via FGAP-041. Land it in both the samples catalog and the active .context.
